LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER THOMAS RUPPEL'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND DECLARATORY RELIEF UNDER THE PUBLIC RECORDS ACT (GOV. CODE 7920.000, ET SEQ.) IS GRANTED. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition And Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The Public Records Act (Gov. Code 7920.000, Et Seq.)
Off Calendar Aug-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (personal)
KARL OLSON, ESQ / ZACHARY E. COLBETH, ESQ. CANNATA O'TOOLE & OLSON LLP (415) 409-8900 kolson@cofolaw.com / zcolbeth@cofolaw.com Attorneys for Petitioner THOMAS RUPPEL
BRANDON S. WALKER, Esq. Deputy Attorney General (916) 210-6395 Brandon.Walker@doj.ca.gov
Attorneys for Respondent Department of Parks & Recreatiofor Defendant.
Carol Nygard, CSR# 4018 Carol Nygard & Associates 877-438-7787 nygardreporting.com Court reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER THOMAS RUPPEL's Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The Public Records Act (Gov. Code 7920.000, Et Seq.).

Petitioner Ruppel's petition for writ of mandate is granted.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle East, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Carol Nygard, CSR# 4018 Carol Nygard & Associates 877-438-7787 nygardreporting.com Reported. (302/RCE)
